results.ExplainText:
Explanation
  1) First, we lock ROW for read on PROD_DB.fact_sales.
  2) Next, we do an all-AMPs RETRIEVE step from PROD_DB.fact_sales by way of an all-rows scan with a condition of ("PROD_DB.fact_sales.sale_date >= DATE - 30") into Spool 2 (all_amps), which is redistributed by hash code to all AMPs. The estimated row count is 520,000 rows.
  3) We do an all-AMPs JOIN step from Spool 2 (Last Use) by way of a RowHash match scan, which is joined to PROD_DB.dim_customer by way of a RowHash match scan. PROD_DB.dim_customer is joined using a product join, with a join condition of ("PROD_DB.fact_sales.customer_id = PROD_DB.dim_customer.customer_id"). The estimated row count is 520,000 rows.
  4) Finally, we send out an END TRANSACTION step to all AMPs involved in processing the request.
  -> The contents of Spool 3 are sent back to the user as the result of statement 1.
